changes.value:
## What

Add an `lsp status` operation to the existing code-context MCP tool that returns structured language/LSP health info. This replaces the hardcoded `get_lsp_servers_for_type()` in `doctor.rs` and becomes the single API that both the /lsp skill and the statusline consume.

The operation queries indexed file extensions from the code-context database, cross-references with the LSP registry (from card 1), and returns which languages are present, which LSPs are installed/missing, and install hints.

### Files to change:
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs` — add `lsp status` op
- New file: `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/lsp_status.rs` — handler
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/doctor.rs` — delete `get_lsp_servers_for_type()` and `detect_project_types()`, use the LSP registry instead
- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` — add `distinct_extensions(conn) -> Result<HashSet<String>>` query function

### Response shape:
```json
{
  "languages": [
    {
      "icon": "🦀",
      "extensions": ["rs"],
      "lsp_server": "rust-analyzer",
      "installed": true,
      "install_hint": null
    },
    {
      "icon": "🐍",
      "extensions": ["py"],
      "lsp_server": "pylsp",
      "installed": false,
      "install_hint": "pip install python-lsp-server"
    }
  ],
  "all_healthy": false
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `code_context` tool accepts `op: "lsp status"` and returns language/LSP JSON
- [ ] Only languages with files in the index appear in the response
- [ ] Each language entry includes icon, extensions, server command, installed bool, install_hint
- [ ] `all_healthy` is true only when every present language has its LSP installed
- [ ] `doctor.rs` no longer has hardcoded server lists — it delegates to the registry

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: `distinct_extensions` returns correct set from test DB
- [ ] Integration test: `lsp status` op returns expected shape
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools`
Add `lsp status` operation to code-context MCP tool
